name: web-pagination
goal: Add pagination to Meetings, People, and Commitments list pages in the web UI, improving performance and UX for large datasets

userStories.title: Backend Pagination Support
userStories.description: Add limit and offset query parameters to /api/meetings, /api/people, and /api/commitments endpoints with consistent response format
acceptanceCriteria: /api/meetings accepts ?limit=25&offset=0 query params
/api/meetings returns { meetings, total, offset, limit } response shape
/api/people accepts ?limit=25&offset=0 query params
/api/people returns { people, total, offset, limit } response shape
/api/commitments accepts ?limit=25&offset=0 query params
/api/commitments returns { commitments, total, offset, limit } response shape
Default limit is 25, max limit is 100
Existing filters apply before pagination (total reflects filtered count)
All tests pass (npm test in backend package)

userStories.title: Frontend - Meetings Pagination
userStories.description: Add pagination to MeetingsIndex page with URL-based page state
acceptanceCriteria: useMeetings({ limit, offset }) hook accepts optional pagination params with defaults
fetchMeetings(params) API function supports pagination params
MeetingsIndex reads page from URL search params using useSearchParams
MeetingsIndex shows 25 items per page
Pagination UI shows at bottom when total > 25 (Previous/Next buttons, page info)
Clicking Next/Previous updates URL and fetches new page
Changing filter tabs (All/Triage/Approved) resets page to 1
Loading skeleton shows while fetching new page
Page state persists on refresh (URL-based)

userStories.title: Frontend - People Pagination
userStories.description: Add pagination to PeopleIndex page with URL-based page state
acceptanceCriteria: usePeople({ limit, offset }) hook accepts optional pagination params with defaults
fetchPeople(params) API function supports pagination params
PeopleIndex reads page from URL search params
PeopleIndex shows 25 items per page
Pagination UI shows at bottom when total > 25
Changing category tabs (Favorites/All/Internal/Customer/User) resets page to 1
Changing commitment filter (?filter=overdue) resets page to 1
Loading skeleton shows while fetching new page
Consistent UX with MeetingsIndex pagination

userStories.title: Frontend - Commitments Pagination
userStories.description: Add pagination to CommitmentsPage with URL-based page state
acceptanceCriteria: useCommitments({ limit, offset, ...filters }) hook accepts pagination params
fetchCommitments(params) API function supports pagination params
CommitmentsPage reads page from URL search params
CommitmentsPage shows 25 items per page
Pagination UI shows at bottom when total > 25
Changing filter tabs (Open/Overdue/This Week/All) resets page to 1
Changing direction tabs (Mine/Theirs/All) resets page to 1
Changing priority tabs (All/High/Medium/Low) resets page to 1
Consistent UX with other paginated pages
